issue.message: Overriding method should call `super.onBackPressed`
`android:windowSplashScreenBackground` requires API level 31 (current min is 26)
`android:windowSplashScreenAnimatedIcon` requires API level 31 (current min is 26)
Avoid passing `null` as the view root (needed to resolve layout parameters on the inflated layout's root element)

Expecting `android:screenOrientation="unspecified"` or `"fullSensor"` for this activity so the user can use the application in any orientation and provide a great experience on Chrome OS devices
Should not restrict activity to fixed orientation. This may not be suitable for different form factors, causing the app to be letterboxed.

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

The attribute `android:allowBackup` is deprecated from Android 12 and higher and may be removed in future versions. Consider adding the attribute `android:dataExtractionRules` specifying an `@xml` resource which configures cloud backups and device transfers on Android 12 and higher.
This folder configuration (`v26`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 26. Merge all the resources in this folder into `mipmap-anydpi`.
Do not place Android context classes in static fields; this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields; this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields; this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields; this is a memory leak

Buttons in button bars should be borderless; use `style="?android:attr/buttonBarButtonStyle"` (and `?android:attr/buttonBarStyle` on the parent)

Custom view ``MapView`` has `setOnTouchListener` called on it but does not override `performClick`
`onTouch` lambda should call `View#performClick` when a click is detected
Custom view `WickedMapView` overrides `onTouchEvent` but not `performClick`

issue.message: Hardcoded string "SuperAdmin mode (toggle parked V1+ services)", should use `@string` resource

issue.message: When you define `paddingStart` you should probably also define `paddingEnd` for right-to-left symmetry
